source.domain: sports equipment, gym/fitness hardware, rehabilitation exercise devices, and adjustable training mechanisms
source.raw_idea_title: Adjustable Olympic Barbell Safety Catch Hook

requirements.part_name: Adjustable Olympic Barbell Safety Catch Hook - single metal part

material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable Olympic Barbell Safety Catch Hook'. The
broader use case is: Power rack attachment for catching a loaded barbell during squats or bench
press The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined steel hook body with rack
pin bore, radiused bar cradle, rear locating boss, and gusseted load path All other product elements
are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Locks into rack uprights at selectable heights and presents a reinforced curved catch surface for
the bar It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines large-radius
contact geometry, offset load-bearing features, multiple coaxial holes, filleted transitions, and
asymmetric reinforcement ribs The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using round
bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the
center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or
fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 67 mm and length 47 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 16 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 26 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 22 mm wide over 39 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M8 holes through the top flat at X=15 mm and X=31 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 4 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single cylindrical body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'axial through bore' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'end counterbores' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'milled reference flat' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'radial clamp holes' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'split relief slit' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'outside edge treatment' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Round features are coaxial unless the requirement explicitly says they are radial or offset.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Adjustable Olympic Barbell Safety Catch Hook'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

source.domain: sports equipment, gym/fitness hardware, rehabilitation exercise devices, and adjustable training mechanisms
source.raw_idea_title: Indexed Cable Machine Pulley Swivel Arm

requirements.part_name: Indexed Cable Machine Pulley Swivel Arm - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Indexed Cable Machine Pulley Swivel Arm'. The
broader use case is: Adjustable functional trainer used in gyms for cable exercises at different
pull angles The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Aluminum swivel yoke arm with
pivot boss, detent holes on an arc, pulley axle supports, and cable clearance cutouts All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Rotates a pulley housing through indexed angular positions while carrying cable
tension It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests curved hole patterns,
forked yoke geometry, concentric pivot features, thin clearance slots, and load-bearing blends
between bosses and arms The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 220 mm x 75 mm x 35 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 12 mm on the length centerline at X=15 mm and X=205 mm, Y=37 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 73 mm long x 12 mm wide through the part, centered at X=110 mm, Y=37 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 55 mm x 25 mm x 4 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.

Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Indexed Cable Machine Pulley Swivel Arm'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

requirements.part_name: Rehabilitation Ankle Resistance Pedal - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Rehabilitation Ankle Resistance Pedal'. The broader
use case is: Physical therapy device for controlled ankle dorsiflexion and plantarflexion exercises
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Contoured pedal plate with heel cup, strap
slots, pivot barrel, resistance anchor holes, and underside stiffening ribs All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Supports the foot while connecting to adjustable resistance bands or a small torque mechanism
It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires ergonomic curved surfaces,
strap apertures, ribbed underside structure, hinge-axis alignment, and multiple anchor points with
smooth edges The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate
stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots,
pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in
the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 48 mm long x 12 mm wide through the part, centered at X=72 mm, Y=40 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 36 mm x 26 mm x 1 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Variable Incline Bench Locking Sector'. The broader
use case is: Adjustable weight bench mechanism for selecting backrest incline angles The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined sector plate with radial tooth pockets,
central pivot bore, mounting holes, stop shoulder, and thickened perimeter All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Provides discrete locking positions for the bench support arm under user and weight load It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests radial arrays, angular indexing
features, variable-thickness profiles, precise pivot relationships, and stress-relief fillets around
notches The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock.
Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets,
lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same

requirements.part_name: Rowing Machine Foot Stretcher Slider - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Rowing Machine Foot Stretcher Slider'. The broader
use case is: Indoor rowing machine foot platform adjustable for different shoe sizes The chosen
deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Sliding carriage block with rail groove, spring-pin
locking holes, footplate mounting bosses, and strap clearance windows All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Slides
along a rail and locks the foot stretcher position while supporting strap and heel forces It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines linear guide geometry, repeated
locking features, offset mounting bosses, rectangular through-windows, and tight functional
clearances The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock.

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

broader use case is: Selectorized dumbbell handle that engages different weight plates based on dial
position The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Cylindrical cam sleeve with
stepped engagement lobes, center bore, detent groove, timing marks, and retaining-ring channels All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Rotates to capture or release plate lugs in a programmed sequence It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests nonuniform rotational profiles,
stepped cam surfaces, concentric grooves, axial feature sequencing, and precise angular
relationships The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock.

use case is: Folding treadmill deck support that allows controlled deck lift and locked storage
position The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Forged or machined hinge block
with twin clevis ears, large pivot bore, stop lugs, mounting slots, and relief pockets All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Carries the pivot joint between the frame and running deck while providing hard
stops for travel limits It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests
clevis symmetry, high-load pivot bores, integrated stop geometry, elongated slots, pocketed mass
reduction, and robust fillet networks The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face
all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments
into that same solid piece.

source.raw_idea_title: Grip-Width Adjustable Lat Pulldown Handle Hub

requirements.part_name: Grip-Width Adjustable Lat Pulldown Handle Hub - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Grip-Width Adjustable Lat Pulldown Handle Hub'. The
broader use case is: Cable attachment handle with sliding grips for different shoulder widths and
exercise variations The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Central hub block with
cable eye boss, opposing dovetail tracks, locking pin holes, scale recesses, and end stops All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Locks two handle arms at selectable lateral positions while transferring cable load
through a central eye It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires
dovetail or keyed sliding geometry, opposing mirrored features, load-centered eyelet modeling,
repeated adjustment holes, and compact clearance details The part is made from low-carbon steel,
ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the
outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as
needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 89 mm and length 113 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 17 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 27 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 29 mm wide over 105 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=37 mm and X=75 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 4 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

requirements.part_name: Continuous Passive Motion Knee Linkage Crank - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Continuous Passive Motion Knee Linkage Crank'. The
broader use case is: Rehabilitation machine that gently cycles a patient's knee through a programmed
range of motion The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Eccentric crank disk with
adjustable radial slot, drive shaft bore, linkage pin boss, counterweight pocket, and clamp split
All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Converts motor rotation into adjustable reciprocating motion for a leg
support linkage It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests eccentric
geometry, radial slots, clamping splits, offset bosses, circular-pocket mass balancing, and motion-
critical reference axes The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round
bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the
center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or
fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 10 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 20 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 17 mm wide over 101 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes

requirements.part_name: Resistance Sled Harness Tow Cleat - single metal part

material.reason: strong cylindrical metal part with threaded and slotted details

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Resistance Sled Harness Tow Cleat'. The broader use
case is: Training sled used for sprint resistance and strength conditioning The chosen deliverable
is only the metal body implied by: Machined tow cleat with rounded strap horn, base bolt pattern,
angled web reinforcement, rope groove, and wear-facing radii All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Anchors a tow
strap or harness line to the sled while allowing quick angle changes under high pulling loads It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines organic strap-contact curves,
angled reinforcement webs, bolt-hole patterns, groove surfaces, and thick-to-thin transitions under
realistic load paths The part is made from 4140 alloy steel, prehard using round bar stock. Start
from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.
